comments:
The State Auditor's Office (SAO)
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.
See RSIN 5.4.018 and 5.4.019 for
Audit Plan records.
series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.
series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.

comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of RSIN
1.1.048.
series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
RSIN 1.1.008 should be used only
for correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another records series on the
agency's approved schedule. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by RSIN 1.1.004; a letter
concerning an audit for that
prescribed by RSIN 1.1.002, etc. See
also RSIN 1.1.011.
series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership,
supervisory, managerial, executive,
administrative, substantive, and other high-
level correspondence in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to the formulation, planning,
implementation, interpretation,
modification, or redefinition of the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.

comments:
See comment to RSIN 1.1.007. See
also RSIN 1.1.010.
series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency.

Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.
series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard to
pending litigation, or opinions
rendered for Public Information Act
Requests. See RSIN 1.1.048,
1.1.020, and 1.1.021.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and
advice that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation. See page ix for
additional guidelines.
series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.

News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, and
audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code), including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.
series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.

Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.

Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.067 for summary
reports compiled from customer
surveys.
series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from customers
or clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency's
performance.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.
series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.

comments:
See RSIN 3.3.030 for internal
personnel training materials.
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training records.
See RSIN 5.4.007.
series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non-personnel
individuals it regulates or serves, including
but not limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in external training
programs.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. See page ix
for more information.
series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.

Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan.

Self-evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.

comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should make certain
records are not part of another
records series listed in this schedule
or, for records series unique to an
agency, are not part of a records
series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions.
The disposal of transitory
information need not be
documented through destruction
signoffs (RSIN 1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (RSIN 1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (RSIN 1.2.014).
series_description:
Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. See page xii for
examples.

Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.068 for reports on
agency performance measures.
CAUTION: The FE + 3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records series
in this schedule if the records series
is needed for documentation of
agency performance measures.
series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.067 for reports
produced from raw data.
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. See RSIN
1.1.064.
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing non-
fiscal reports.
series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) – Raw Data

comments:
AC = September 1 of odd-numbered
calendar years.
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.
series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.065 for raw data used
to produce reports.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs. For agencies that have
had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of
archival coding, A, R, or E. See page
ix for more information.
series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not noted
elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.064 for documentation
used to produce reports on agency
performance measures.
series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.

comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures,
see RSIN 1.1.064.
series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.

Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code, 552.010.

Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.

comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation
in which the state agency is a party,
see RSIN 1.1.048.
series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal
documents requiring recipient to appear in
court to testify, or to produce records to
be used in litigation.

comments:
CAUTION: If an accident occurs to
any person covered by a signed
waiver of liability, it must be
retained for the same period as
accident reports. See RSIN 5.4.001
and 5.4.014.
series_description:
Waivers of liability, including statements
signed by volunteers acknowledging non-
entitlement to benefits, agreeing to abide
by state agency policies, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: If destruction
authorizations are maintained as
part of RSIN 1.2.010 (Records
Disposition Logs), then longer
retention period applies.
series_description:
Documents authorizing final disposition of
records under a certified records retention
schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to an agency form or
any associated design or design
modification requests.

comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Note: SOAH's Records Management
Officer retains copy.
series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.

comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.
series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.

comments: Obsolete record.
series_description:
Agency copy of form RMD 106 granting a
state agency the authorization to store
records at the State Records Center.

Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.

Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

Also includes documentation for disaster
recovery services provided by other
entities.

Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113)
or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff to
access the media, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will retain
a copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic evaluation
to determine if the publication
merits further retention.
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.8).
series_description:
One copy of each state publication as
defined on page xi of this schedule, except
a publication that is subject to a different
retention period in this schedule. For
example, a meeting agenda (see RSIN
1.1.058) also meets the definition, but it
must be retained permanently; RSIN
1.1.004, 1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and
4.5.003, which also meet the definition,
are closely associated with the
appropriations process and must be
retained AC + 6.

comments:
See RSIN 1.3.001 for final State
Publications created from
development files.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major
is defined by both the publication's
authorship and its impact on Texas
and Texans. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant value
as evidence of agency programs as
well as the potential for re-use, may
be archival.
series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc.
This includes all work performed both
inside and outside the agency.

comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.
series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.

comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.
series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.
series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated processing
files.

comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.
series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include but
are not limited to: user guides, system or
sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts, program
flow charts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and
other records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.

Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates to
application files; and security logs.

comments:
The disposal of monitoring records
that are automatically overwritten
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (RSIN
1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (RSIN 1.2.010), but agencies
should establish procedures
governing disposal of these records
as part of its records management
plan (RSIN 1.2.014).
series_description:
Hardware and software components for
monitoring agency’s computer system
resources and performance.

Records documenting usage, costs, billing,
cost recovery, budgeting, and
administrative functions of computer
usage and data processing services for
individual units/departments/divisions in
an agency.
series_title:
Chargeback Records to
Data Processing Services
Users

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records.
See RSIN 2.1.009.
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.

Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile
data sets submitted for processing.

comments:
For quality control records related
to non-IT procedures, see RSIN
5.2.018.
series_description:
Information verifying the quality of system,
hardware, or software operations. Records
include but are not limited to: records of
errors or failures and the loss of data
resulting from such failures;
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error-free processing; checks of
changes put into production; transaction
histories; and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.

comments:
The disposal of internet history
records need not be documented
through destruction authorizations
(RSIN 1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (RSIN 1.2.010), but
agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (RSIN 1.2.014).
series_description:
A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user-specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state-
owned computer.

Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.

Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests. See RSIN
3.1.042. Note: Records maintained
in CAPPS Recruit.
series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.021.
series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.

comments:
Note: Maintained in CAPPS and
Employees Retirement System
(ERS).
series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, vision, dental, disability,
and other types of insurance or benefits
offered by the State of Texas to its
employees.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.014.
series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

comments:
Note: May be maintained as part of
RSIN 5.1.001a.
series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.

comments:
Note: May be maintained as part of
RSIN 5.1.001b.
series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include criminal
history checks; see RSIN 3.1.026.
Does not include drug screening
test results; see RSIN 3.1.040a/b/c.
Does not include pre-employments
skills tests; see RSIN 3.3.027 and
3.3.028. Note: Includes RSINs
3.1.002, 3.1.012, and 3.3.027.

CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor (DOL). See
RSIN 1.1.048.
series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
grievances or complaints against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee.

comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under RSIN
3.1.021, all documentation from
this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained
for the minimum retention period
described by RSIN 3.1.021. Note:
Maintained as part of RSIN 3.1.002.
series_description:
Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are
imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure to
improve performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May
include cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records
of an employee, including pay grade,
position classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.

comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information. Note: Maintained
as part of RSIN 3.1.002.
series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.

comments:
Note: I-9 forms are filed separate
from personnel records maintained
as part of RSIN 3.1.002.
series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must be
kept as long as the former
employee verification records. See
RSIN 3.3.011. Note: Maintained in
CAPPS.
series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security number,
and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code 552.024.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the ADA
(Americans with Disabilities Act).

comments:
Note: Maintained in CAPPS or
Uniform Statewide
Payroll/Personnel System (USPS).
series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.

Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.

Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS or USPS.
series_description:
Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate."

Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS or USPS.
series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal account.

Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.

Affirmative action plans for regular
employees.

comments:
Note: Information may be accessed
through ERS website.

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.

comments:
See RSIN 3.1.038. Note: Maintained
in CAPPS or USPS, or stored at the
State Records Center in hard-copy
format.
series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment. Includes: name; social
security number; exact dates of
employment; last known address; and
most recent public access authorization.

Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
3.1.002.
series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.

Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

comments:
Note: Maintained as part of RSIN
4.1.001 in CAPPS.
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.

comments:
Note: Maintained by each individual
agency division.
series_description:
Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.

comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated. Note: Maintained as part
of RSIN 3.1.014.
series_description:
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion
or transfer, including validation records.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.043 for external
training records. See RSIN 3.1.027
for individual employee training
records.
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training records.
See RSIN 5.4.007.
series_description:
Records documenting the planning,
development, implementation,
administration and evaluation of in-house
training programs, including but not
limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in in-house training
programs.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity)
reports.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances at end of fiscal year, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.

Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.
series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests

comments: Note: Maintained in CAPPS.
series_description:
Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.

Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.

comments:
CAUTION: This series does not
include fiscal reports created to
fulfill grant requirements. See RSIN
4.7.008.
series_description:
Internal and external periodic fiscal
management reports and associated
worksheets, including federal financial
reports, salary reports, HUB reports,
investment performance reports, etc.

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biennial or annual
narrative report as described in
RSIN 1.1.066, then these annual
financial reports must be marked
with Archival Code A. The archival
requirement, when a biennial or
annual narrative report is not
produced, is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
series_description:
Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required by
the General Appropriations Act (100 Day
Report).

Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).

Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.

Records of accounts deemed uncollectible,
including write-off authorizations and
returned checks, warrants, and drafts.

Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of agency
owned equipment and property.

Records documenting requests for
payment of a sum of money according to
the terms of a policy or contract.

Records documenting financial obligations
of a state agency that are not payable
within one year of the date of the balance
sheet, including debentures, loans,
deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension
obligations.

comments:
Note: Includes USAS signature cards
and Letters of Authorization
series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.

Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers and
revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.

comments:
See related RSIN 5.3.007a/b/c for
bid documentation. See RSIN
5.2.028 for building construction
contracts. See RSIN 5.1.017 for
contract logs.
series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.

comments:
See related RSIN 5.3.007a/b/c for
bid documentation. See RSIN
5.2.028 for building construction
contracts. See RSIN 5.1.017 for
contract logs.
series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.

Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.

Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.

Liability, theft, fire, health, life,
automobile, and other policies for
government property and personnel
including supporting documentation
relevant to the implementation,
modification, renewal, or replacement of
policies.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.

Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that establish
standard office procedures for an agency,
e.g. agency style manuals, telephone
protocols, mail room procedures, print
shop and photocopy ordering instructions.

Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service (USPS) or by private
couriers.

List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements, including general obligation,
land lease, utilities, and construction
contracts.

Records documenting building space
requests and approvals. Information on
request forms may include but is not
limited to: the building and room; the
reason for the change; parties involved in
the request; date of request and approval.

comments:
See RSIN 5.2.008 for the
maintenance logs of individual
pieces of equipment.
series_description:
Records documenting the inventorying,
maintenance, usage, and disposal of
supplies, equipment, and property of a
state agency. Includes lost, stolen, and
damage reports.

comments:
For service agreements or contracts
related to equipment repairs and
service, retain in accordance with
RSIN 5.1.001a/b. For vehicle
maintenance records, see RSIN
5.6.003.
series_description:
Equipment history file, which may include
logbooks and requests for installation,
moves, service, repair, etc.
series_title:
Inspection, Repair, and
Maintenance Records –
Equipment

series_description:
Updates agency portion of the inventory
listing and adds, changes, transfers, or
deletes items from inventory.

comments:
See RSIN 5.2.006 for annual
inventory listing.
series_description:
Listing shows all additions, changes,
deletions, and transfer times for the
monthly processing period.

Requests or work orders for repairs or
maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or
equipment completed by internal agency
personnel or external service providers.

Reports summarizing efficiency of facility
space utilization, which may include data
on room usage, demand, allocation, and
capacity.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or before August
31, 2015.

Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.)

Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received,
and similar data on procurement status.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.020 and 1.1.021 for
public information requests.
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to request for
proposal or bid, the request for
information documentation should
be retained in accordance with RSIN
5.3.007a/b/c.
series_description:
Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or services by
direct purchase or bid.

W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer
identification number (TIN) for reporting
information to the Internal Revenue
Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms
received by a state agency from vendors.

comments:
See RSIN 5.4.014a/b for non-
employee accidents.
series_description:
Accident or occupational disease reports
(by supervisors and employees) and other
associated reports required to be
submitted to the State Office of Risk
Management (SORM)or its predecessors or
maintained internally on accident
frequency.
series_title:
Occupational Accident
Reports and Associated
Documentation

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in
cases of emergency.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
inspection reports of building
construction. See RSIN 5.2.028.
series_description:
Fire, safety, and other inspection records
of agency facilities and equipment,
including orders issued by inspectors to
correct deficiencies in compliance with any
code or regulations.

Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated
systems.

comments:
See RSIN 5.4.017 for records related
to responding or recovering from an
emergency.
series_description:
Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code,
412.054) to mitigate the damage of
potential events that could endanger an
agency’s ability to function. Includes
disaster preparedness, response, and
recovery plans.

Reports of accidents to adults on state
property or in any other situation in which
the state agency could be a party to a
lawsuit.

Reports of accidents to minors on state
property or in any other situation in which
the state agency could be a party to a
lawsuit.

comments:
See RSIN 5.4.013 for Continuity of
Operations Plans (COOP).
CAUTION: If grant monies are
received to assist with the response
or recovery, the retention
requirements for this series may
vary depending on the specific
funding agency.
series_description:
This series documents the events and
damages to institutional property due to
storms, riots, fires, droughts, floods, and
other events affecting citizens and facilities
within the jurisdiction of the institution.
This series may include but is not limited
to: diaries; logs; reports; photographs;
notes which indicate or document what
happened, when, and where; and related
documentation and correspondence.

comments:
See RSIN 1.1.002 for individual
Audit records not related to the
development of the Audit Plan.
series_description:
Includes working papers and agency risk
assessment used to develop the plan, per
Texas Internal Auditing Act requirement.

comments: See RSIN 4.1.001 for telephone bills.
series_description:
Telephone logs created for internal
documentation purposes; station activity
reports of internal listings of
incoming/outgoing telephone activity to
individual telephone stations; operator call
transfers; summary detail reports; and
system activity reports of internal listings
of all incoming/outgoing agency telephone
activity.

comments:
The billing agency will maintain all
long distance TEX-AN records and
will provide each using agency its
bill summary of centralized capitol
complex telephone service without
call detail records. For these bill
summaries, SEE item number
4.7.011. SEE item number 5.5.001
for billing detail from carriers other
than TEX-AN.
series_description:
In addition to summary detail, includes any
accompanying detailed listing of long
distance calls.

comments:
Note: Incoming correspondence
from DPS relating to parking,
updates on SOAH’s parking
assignments, copies of applications
for assignments.
series_description:
Records documenting issuance of parking
permits and assignments.

comments:
Note: All weekly dockets stored in
PDF format.
series_description:
Final version of published weekly dockets
for all SOAH offices.
series_title:
Weekly Docket Schedules
(Administrative License
Revocation (ALR) and
General Docket)

comments:
Note: The electronic record is
received daily from DPS.
series_description:
Electronic file from DPS containing
information for request to docket of ALR
cases.

comments:
Note: All SOAH-issued orders are to
be retained. See AIN 160 for
retention information for ALR
orders.
series_description:
Includes all ALR case file pleadings,
exhibits, and motions.

comments:
Note: SOAH will maintain the file
and original recording(s) of
proceedings. A copy of the file and
recording(s) will be made available
for review by the parties or a
reviewing court, if necessary.
Records may contain a copy of the
decision if received from a county
or district court. Cases appealed
prior to October 1, 2020, are stored
in paper format. Cases appealed
October 1, 2020, and thereafter are
stored in electronic format.
series_description:
Includes all ALR case file pleadings,
exhibits, motions, SOAH-issued orders, and
the decision received from the county or
district court for appealed ALR cases.
series_title:
ALR Appealed Case Files -
Closed and Judicial Court
Orders

comments:
Note: Orders issued prior to
October 1, 2020, are stored in
paper format. Orders issued
October 1, 2020, and thereafter are
stored in electronic format.
series_description:
Includes all SOAH-issued ALR orders
contained in the records of the proceeding.

Includes all digital recordings of the ALR
proceedings that are maintained by SOAH.

Includes all digital recordings of the ALR
proceedings that are maintained by SOAH
for appealed case files.

comments:
Note: Pursuant to SOAH rules, after
certain exhibits and/or transcripts
are returned to the referring agency
or governmental body, they are no
longer maintained by SOAH.
series_description:
Includes all pleadings, motions, certified
exhibits (including hard-copy exhibits not
filed in SOAH's case management system),
SOAH-issued documents (e.g., orders,
letters, proposals for decision, final orders,
etc.), transcripts (if any), and a copy of the
digital recordings of the proceedings (if
any) returned to the referring agency or
governmental body after SOAH has
concluded its involvement in the case.
series_title:
General Docket Case File -
Returned to Referring
Agency or Governmental
Body

comments:
Note: Parts of this record series
may be confidential and subject to
legal review. This record series
includes contested cases that are
referred to Alternative Dispute
Resolution (ADR) during the course
of the administrative hearing
process. See AIN 206. ADR
document submissions by parties
follow AIN 207.
series_description:
Includes all pleadings, motions, certified
exhibits (excluding hard-copy exhibits
returned to the referring agency or
governmental body), and transcripts (if
any) stored in SOAH's case management
system and all SOAH-issued documents
(e.g., orders, letters, proposals for
decision, final orders, etc.) contained in
SOAH's case management system.

comments:
Note: Due to federal confidentiality
laws, after Hearing Officer's final
action in the case, the case file is
returned to the Texas Education
Agency (TEA) and all administrative
case records maintained by SOAH
are destroyed.
series_description:
Includes all pleadings, motions, exhibits,
SOAH-issued documents (e.g., orders,
letters, decisions, etc.), and transcripts.
series_title:
Individuals with Disabilities
Education Act (IDEA) Due
Process Hearing Case Files

series_description:
Includes all digital recordings of the
General Docket proceedings that are
maintained by SOAH.

comments:
Note: This record series applies only
to cases that are referred to SOAH
for ADR. Contested cases that are
referred to ADR during the course
of the administrative hearing
process are classified under AIN
165.
series_description:
Includes documents associated with
docketing, setting, and completing the ADR
process at SOAH.

comments:
Note: This record series includes
any ADR documents submitted by
parties in the course of ADR
proceedings at SOAH. Includes
documents submitted in AINs 165
and 206.
series_description:
Includes confidential documents submitted
to the Mediator for ADR proceedings.
